observation.summary: Several package and boundary workflows executed Go tests and source boundary checks, but remained green-check-only CI without evidence artifacts.

hypothesis.summary: A compact workflow-command evidence manifest can make these legacy package gates observable without moving their domain semantics into one large tool.

execution.summary: Run packageworkflowevidence from each registered workflow to prove the expected command fragments remain present, then upload strict JSON artifacts.

evaluation.summary: workflowevidence must move registered package workflows from accepted_gap to covered, and packageworkflowevidence must fail if a required command fragment disappears.

retrospective.summary: Package workflows become evidence-producing orchestration while existing domain-specific tests and source gates keep their ownership.

evidence.proves: registered package workflow command fragments are present
public CI reports package workflows as covered evidence surfaces
